kitchen.title: Kitchen Renovation
kitchen.description: Complete kitchen remodeling with modern designs, custom cabinetry, and premium countertops.
kitchen.longDescription: Transform your kitchen into the heart of your home with our comprehensive renovation services. From custom cabinetry and premium countertops to state-of-the-art appliances and innovative storage solutions, we create kitchens that are both beautiful and functional.

bathroom.title: Bathroom Renovation
bathroom.description: Transform your bathroom into a spa-like retreat with luxury fixtures and finishes.
bathroom.longDescription: Create your personal sanctuary with our bathroom renovation expertise. We specialize in luxury spa-inspired designs, modern fixtures, custom tile work, and innovative storage solutions that maximize both style and functionality.

wholeHouse.title: Whole House Renovation
wholeHouse.description: Full-scale home transformations from concept to completion.

basement.title: Basement Remodeling
basement.description: Convert your basement into functional living space. Ranked 3rd in Best of Vancouver.
basement.longDescription: Unlock the full potential of your home with our award-winning basement remodeling services. Whether you want a home theater, gym, guest suite, or home office, we transform underutilized basements into beautiful, functional living spaces.

cabinet.title: Cabinet Refacing

cabinet.longDescription: Give your kitchen a stunning new look without the cost of a full renovation. Our cabinet refacing services include new doors, drawer fronts, and hardware, transforming your kitchen's appearance while preserving your existing cabinet boxes.

commercial.longDescription: Elevate your business space with our commercial renovation expertise. We handle office build-outs, retail store renovations, restaurant redesigns, and more, delivering professional results that enhance your brand and customer experience.

kitchen.q1: How long does a kitchen renovation typically take?
kitchen.a1: A typical kitchen renovation takes 4-8 weeks depending on the scope. Simple updates like cabinet refacing may take 1-2 weeks, while complete gut renovations with custom cabinetry can take 8-12 weeks.
kitchen.q2: What is the average cost of a kitchen renovation in {area}?
kitchen.a2: Kitchen renovations in {area} typically range from $15,000 to $75,000+ depending on size and finishes. We provide free detailed quotes after an in-home consultation.
kitchen.q3: Do I need permits for my kitchen renovation?
kitchen.a3: Permits are required for electrical, plumbing, and structural changes. We handle all permit applications and ensure your renovation meets {area} building codes.

bathroom.q1: How long does a bathroom renovation in {area} take?
bathroom.a1: Most bathroom renovations in {area} take 2-4 weeks. A simple cosmetic update may take 1 week, while a complete renovation with layout changes can take 4-6 weeks.
bathroom.q2: Can I use my bathroom during renovation?
bathroom.a2: Typically no, but we work efficiently to minimize disruption. If you have multiple bathrooms, we can help you plan around the renovation schedule.
bathroom.q3: What waterproofing do you use for bathrooms?
bathroom.a3: We use industry-leading waterproofing systems including Schluter and RedGard to ensure your bathroom is properly protected against moisture damage.

whole-house.q1: Can I live in my home during a whole house renovation in {area}?
whole-house.a1: It depends on the scope. For major renovations in {area}, we often recommend temporary relocation for safety and efficiency. We can help coordinate phased work if staying is preferred.
whole-house.q2: How do you manage a whole house renovation project?
whole-house.a2: We assign a dedicated project manager who coordinates all trades, maintains the schedule, and provides regular updates. You have a single point of contact throughout.
whole-house.q3: Do I need permits for a whole house renovation in {area}?
whole-house.a3: Yes, whole house renovations typically require multiple permits for structural, electrical, and plumbing work. We handle all {area} permit applications and inspections.

basement.q1: Do I need a permit to finish my basement in {area}?
basement.a1: Yes, basement finishing requires building permits in {area}. We handle all permit applications and inspections as part of our service.
basement.q2: How do you handle basement moisture issues?
basement.a2: We assess moisture levels and implement appropriate solutions including drainage improvements, vapor barriers, and proper insulation before finishing.
basement.q3: Can I add a bathroom to my basement?
basement.a3: Yes, we can add bathrooms to basements. If below the sewer line, we install sewage ejector systems. All plumbing meets {area} building codes.

cabinet.q1: What is the difference between refacing and replacing cabinets?
cabinet.a1: Refacing keeps your existing cabinet boxes and replaces doors, drawer fronts, and hardware. It costs 40-50% less than full replacement and takes less time.
cabinet.q2: How long does cabinet refacing take in {area}?
cabinet.a2: Cabinet refacing in {area} typically takes 3-5 days for an average kitchen. Your kitchen remains functional throughout most of the process.
cabinet.a3: Most solid wood and plywood cabinet boxes can be refaced if they are structurally sound. We assess your cabinets during a free in-home consultation in {area}.

commercial.q1: Do you work during business hours?
commercial.a1: We offer flexible scheduling including evenings and weekends to minimize disruption to your business operations. We can phase work to keep parts of your space operational.
commercial.q2: Are you experienced with {area} commercial building codes?
commercial.a2: Yes, our team is experienced with {area} commercial building codes, accessibility requirements, and fire safety regulations. We ensure full compliance with all commercial standards.
commercial.q3: Can you handle tenant improvements in {area}?
commercial.a3: Yes, we specialize in tenant improvements (TI) for offices, retail spaces, and restaurants in {area}. We work with landlords and property managers on approval processes.

homeFaq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
homeFaq.subtitle: Common questions about our renovation services

showroomPage.heroSubtitle: Experience renovation materials, designs, and finishes firsthand
showroomPage.ctaDescription: Book a free consultation at our showroom. Our design team will help you explore options and plan your renovation.
showroomPage.faq1q: Do I need an appointment to visit the showroom?
showroomPage.faq1a: Walk-ins are welcome, but we recommend booking an appointment so our design team can dedicate time to discuss your project in detail.
showroomPage.faq2q: What can I see at the Reno Stars showroom?
showroomPage.faq2a: Our showroom features material samples, cabinet displays, countertop options, tile selections, and design inspiration for kitchen, bathroom, and whole house renovations.
showroomPage.faq3q: Is there parking available at the showroom?
showroomPage.faq3a: Yes, free parking is available directly in front of our showroom location in Richmond.
showroomPage.faq4q: Can I bring my renovation plans to the showroom?
showroomPage.faq4a: Absolutely! Bring your floor plans, photos, or inspiration images. Our team can review them and provide professional recommendations during your visit.
showroomPage.appointmentPrefix: Welcome to schedule a visit to our Renovation showroom

projectsFaqs.q1: How do I get started with a renovation project?
projectsFaqs.a1: Start with a free consultation — contact us online or visit our showroom. We'll discuss your vision, assess the space, and provide a detailed quote and timeline.
projectsFaqs.q2: Can I see examples of your past work?
projectsFaqs.a2: Yes! Browse our portfolio above to see before-and-after photos of completed kitchen, bathroom, and whole house renovation projects across Metro Vancouver.
projectsFaqs.q3: Do you handle permits and inspections?
projectsFaqs.a3: Yes, we manage all necessary permits and coordinate inspections as part of our full-service renovation process, so you don't have to worry about any of the paperwork.

designFaqs.q1: What does the design consultation include?
designFaqs.a1: Our design consultation includes a discussion of your vision, space assessment, material recommendations, 3D visualization when applicable, and a detailed project plan with timeline and budget estimate.
designFaqs.q2: Do you offer 3D renderings of renovation designs?
designFaqs.a2: Yes, we provide 3D visualizations for many projects so you can see exactly how your renovated space will look before any work begins.
designFaqs.q3: How much does a design consultation cost?
designFaqs.a3: Our initial design consultations are free. We believe in helping you explore your options before making any commitment.

step1.title: Initial Consultation
step1.subtitle: Discovery
points: Initial contact via phone, email, Whatsapp, WeChat, or SMS
Confirm renovation areas: kitchen, living room, bathroom, or whole house
Style preference confirmation
Material budget and positioning
Understand renovation needs and expectations
Obtain floor plans and site photos (if available)
step1.faqAnswer: We begin with an initial consultation where you can reach us by phone, email, WhatsApp, WeChat, or SMS. We'll discuss your renovation needs, style preferences, budget, and gather any existing floor plans or photos.

step2.title: Tailored Assessment & Quote
step2.subtitle: On-Site Measurement (if needed) & Transparent Pricing
points: Professional on-site measurement for precise dimensions (if needed)
A comprehensive design proposal illustrating your future space
Clearly defined scope of work, project timeline, permit requirements, and material specifications
A clear and transparent quotation aligned with the proposed design and scope, ensuring full clarity and predictability throughout the process
step2.faqAnswer: We provide professional on-site measurement when needed and create a comprehensive design proposal for your space. You'll receive a clearly defined scope of work with timeline, permit requirements, and a transparent quotation aligned with the proposed design.

step3.title: Confirmation & Contract
step3.subtitle: Finalizing Details & Project Initiation
points: Invite clients to showroom for in-person consultation
Address questions and optimize design details
Confirm design proposal and material samples
Finalize the payment schedule and assist with delivery scheduling
Sign construction contract and collect deposit
Secure the project start date
Establish project team: Project Manager, Construction Manager, Coordinator
step3.faqAnswer: We invite you to our showroom to review material samples and finalize design details. After confirming the payment schedule and signing the contract, we secure your project start date and establish a dedicated project team including a Project Manager, Construction Manager, and Coordinator.

step4.title: Execution & Management
step4.subtitle: Fully Coordinated & Easy Communication
points: A unified project team managing all aspects of your project
No direct interaction with workers needed
Coordinated construction progress to ensure efficiency and consistency
Assistance with delivery scheduling and on-site coordination
Consistent quality control throughout the entire construction process
Fast response and efficient resolution of any issues
step4.faqAnswer: A unified project team manages all aspects of your project. You won't need to interact with workers directly—we coordinate construction progress, delivery scheduling, and maintain consistent quality control throughout. Any issues are resolved quickly and efficiently.

step5.title: Walkthrough & Aftercare
step5.subtitle: Final Checks & Lasting Support
points: Final walkthrough to confirm all work meets expectations
New home handover
Ongoing support for maintenance during warranty period
step5.faqAnswer: We conduct a final walkthrough with you to confirm all work meets your expectations, then hand over your new home. We provide ongoing support for maintenance during the warranty period.

hero.title: Kitchen Renovation Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: Real pricing from completed kitchen renovations across Greater Vancouver. Every number below comes from actual projects — not industry estimates.

tiers.title: Kitchen Renovation Cost by Tier
tiers.subtitle: Based on our completed projects across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Budget-Friendly
budget.description: Cabinet refacing or stock cabinets, laminate countertops, basic backsplash, and standard fixtures. Ideal for condos and townhouses looking for a fresh look without structural changes.

midRange.title: Mid-Range
midRange.description: Custom or semi-custom cabinets, quartz countertops, tile backsplash, updated lighting, and new flooring. The most popular choice for family homes.

highEnd.title: High-End
highEnd.description: Fully custom cabinetry, premium stone countertops, waterfall islands, high-end appliances, structural modifications, and designer finishes. For homeowners who want the best.

factors.title: What Affects Your Kitchen Renovation Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

cabinetry.title: Cabinetry (30–40% of budget)
cabinetry.description: The biggest cost driver. Stock cabinets start around /linear foot, while custom options can exceed /linear foot. Shaker-style white cabinets are the most popular choice in Vancouver.

countertops.title: Countertops (10–15% of budget)
countertops.description: Laminate starts at /sq ft, quartz runs –150/sq ft, and natural stone can exceed /sq ft. Waterfall island edges add –2,000 to the total.

layout.title: Layout Changes
layout.description: Moving plumbing, gas lines, or removing walls significantly increases costs. A layout change can add ,000–15,000 depending on structural requirements and permits.

appliances.title: Appliances
appliances.description: Budget-friendly packages start at ,000–5,000. Mid-range brands like Bosch or KitchenAid run ,000–15,000. Premium brands like Sub-Zero or Wolf can exceed ,000.

plumbing.title: Plumbing & Electrical
plumbing.description: Relocating plumbing or adding circuits adds ,000–8,000. Older Vancouver homes may need electrical panel upgrades to support modern appliances.

permits.title: Permits & Inspections
permits.description: City of Vancouver building permits for kitchen renovations typically cost –1,000. Required when changing plumbing, electrical, gas, or structural elements.

realProjects.title: Real Kitchen Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed kitchen projects with actual budgets and timelines

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips for Your Kitchen Renovation

tip1.title: Keep the Existing Layout
tip1.description: Avoiding plumbing and gas line relocations can save ,000–15,000. Work with your existing kitchen footprint where possible.

tip2.title: Choose Quartz Over Natural Stone
tip2.description: Quartz countertops are more durable, require no sealing, and cost 20–40% less than granite or marble. Plus, they look stunning.

tip3.title: Semi-Custom Cabinets
tip3.description: Semi-custom cabinets offer the look of fully custom at 40–60% of the price. You get to choose door styles, finishes, and configurations.

tip4.title: Plan During Off-Season
tip4.description: Winter months (November–February) are slower for renovators in Vancouver. You may get better pricing and faster timelines by starting then.

cta.heading: Get Your Free Kitchen Renovation Quote
cta.subtitle: Tell us about your project and receive a detailed estimate within 48 hours — no obligation.

faq.q1: How much does a kitchen renovation cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Kitchen renovation costs in Vancouver typically range from $15,000 to $72,000+ based on our completed projects. Budget renovations (cabinet updates, basic countertops) start around $15,000–$27,000, mid-range renovations with custom cabinets and quartz countertops run $28,000–$38,000, and high-end transformations with premium materials can exceed $40,000.
faq.q2: How long does a kitchen renovation take in Vancouver?
faq.a2: Most kitchen renovations in Vancouver take 3 to 6 weeks from demolition to completion. Simple cosmetic updates can be done in 3–4 weeks, while complex renovations involving layout changes, custom cabinetry, or structural work may take 8–10 weeks.
faq.q3: Do I need a permit for a kitchen renovation in Vancouver?
faq.a3: You need a City of Vancouver building permit if your renovation involves changes to plumbing, electrical, gas lines, or structural elements like removing walls. Cosmetic updates like new cabinets, countertops, and backsplash typically do not require permits. Permit costs range from $200 to $1,000.
faq.q4: What is the best countertop material for a kitchen renovation?
faq.a4: Quartz is the most popular countertop choice in our Vancouver kitchen renovations. It offers excellent durability, requires no sealing (unlike granite), resists stains, and comes in a wide variety of colours and patterns. Prices range from $80 to $150 per square foot installed.
faq.q5: Can I renovate my kitchen while living in my home?
faq.a5: Yes, most of our clients continue living in their homes during kitchen renovations. We help set up a temporary kitchen area and schedule work to minimize disruption. Expect to be without a functioning kitchen for 2–4 weeks depending on the scope of work.

hero.title: Bathroom Renovation Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: Real pricing from completed bathroom renovations across Greater Vancouver. Every number comes from actual projects — not industry estimates.

tiers.title: Bathroom Renovation Cost by Tier
tiers.subtitle: Based on our completed projects across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Budget-Friendly
budget.description: Standard tile, basic vanity, updated fixtures, and fresh paint. Ideal for a single bathroom refresh in condos or townhouses without moving plumbing.

midRange.title: Mid-Range
midRange.description: Porcelain or large-format tile, custom vanity, frameless glass shower enclosure, modern fixtures, and heated flooring. The most popular choice for family homes.

highEnd.title: High-End / Multi-Bathroom
highEnd.description: Premium tile, curbless showers, freestanding tubs, custom cabinetry, smart fixtures, and structural modifications. Often includes renovating 2-3+ bathrooms simultaneously.

factors.title: What Affects Your Bathroom Renovation Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

tiles.title: Tile & Flooring (25–35% of budget)
tiles.description: Standard ceramic tile starts at $5/sq ft, porcelain runs $8–15/sq ft, and natural stone can exceed $25/sq ft. Large-format tiles reduce grout lines but cost more to install.

shower.title: Shower / Tub Configuration
shower.description: A tub-to-shower conversion costs $3,000–8,000. Curbless (zero-threshold) showers require custom waterproofing and sloped subfloor, adding $2,000–5,000 vs a standard shower base.

vanity.title: Vanity & Countertop
vanity.description: Stock vanities start at $500–1,500. Semi-custom with quartz top runs $2,000–4,000. Fully custom floating vanities with integrated sinks can exceed $6,000.

plumbing.title: Plumbing Relocation
plumbing.description: Moving drain lines or supply pipes adds $2,000–6,000. Older homes may need new shut-off valves or supply line upgrades. Keeping existing plumbing layout saves significantly.

waterproofing.title: Waterproofing
waterproofing.description: Proper waterproofing with membrane systems (Schluter, Laticrete) costs $1,500–3,500 for a standard shower. Essential in Vancouver's climate to prevent moisture damage behind walls.

permits.title: Permits & Inspections
permits.description: Building permits for bathroom renovations cost $200–800 in Metro Vancouver. Required when moving plumbing, adding new fixtures, or making structural changes.

realProjects.title: Real Bathroom Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed bathroom projects with actual budgets and timelines

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips for Your Bathroom Renovation

tip1.title: Keep Existing Plumbing Layout
tip1.description: Relocating drain lines is one of the biggest cost drivers. Keeping the toilet, shower, and sink in their current positions can save $3,000–6,000.

tip2.title: Choose Large-Format Porcelain Tile
tip2.description: Fewer grout lines mean less labour and a cleaner look. 24x24 or 12x24 porcelain tiles are durable, water-resistant, and more affordable than natural stone.

tip3.title: Renovate Multiple Bathrooms Together
tip3.description: If you need more than one bathroom done, doing them simultaneously saves 10–20% vs separate projects. Contractors can share mobilization costs and bulk-buy materials.

tip4.title: Invest in Waterproofing
tip4.description: Skimping on waterproofing leads to costly repairs later. Proper membrane systems add $1,500–3,500 upfront but prevent $10,000+ in water damage down the road.

cta.heading: Get Your Free Bathroom Renovation Quote
cta.subtitle: Tell us about your project and receive a detailed estimate within 48 hours — no obligation.

faq.q1: How much does a bathroom renovation cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Bathroom renovation costs in Vancouver range from $10,000 to $60,000+ based on our completed projects. A single budget-friendly bathroom starts around $10,000–$20,000, mid-range renovations with custom vanity and glass shower run $20,000–$35,000, and high-end or multi-bathroom projects can exceed $40,000.
faq.q2: How long does a bathroom renovation take?
faq.a2: A single bathroom renovation typically takes 2–5 weeks. Simple cosmetic updates can be done in 2–3 weeks, while projects involving plumbing relocation, curbless showers, or structural changes may take 4–8 weeks. Multi-bathroom projects take 5–11 weeks.
faq.q3: Can I convert my bathtub to a walk-in shower?
faq.a3: Yes, tub-to-shower conversions are one of our most popular requests. The cost ranges from $3,000–$8,000 depending on whether you choose a standard shower base or curbless design. Curbless showers require custom waterproofing and a sloped subfloor.
faq.q4: What is curbless shower and why is it more expensive?
faq.a4: A curbless (zero-threshold) shower has no step or lip at the entry, creating a seamless floor transition. It requires custom waterproofing membrane, a precisely sloped subfloor, and linear drain installation, adding $2,000–$5,000 vs a standard shower base. It is popular for accessibility and modern aesthetics.
faq.q5: Should I renovate multiple bathrooms at the same time?
faq.a5: Yes, renovating multiple bathrooms simultaneously saves 10–20% compared to separate projects. Contractors can share mobilization costs, coordinate trades more efficiently, and buy materials in bulk. We have completed many 2–3 bathroom projects across Greater Vancouver.

hero.title: Whole House Renovation Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: Real pricing from completed whole-home renovations across Greater Vancouver. From condo refreshes to full house transformations — every number comes from actual projects.

tiers.title: Whole House Renovation Cost by Tier
tiers.subtitle: Based on our completed projects across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Cosmetic Refresh
budget.description: Paint throughout, new flooring, updated light fixtures, bathroom refresh (vanity, mirror, hardware), and minor kitchen updates. Ideal for condos and rental properties needing a modern update without structural work.

midRange.title: Comprehensive Renovation
midRange.description: Full kitchen renovation with new cabinets and countertops, bathroom remodels, new flooring throughout, fresh paint, updated electrical and plumbing fixtures, and new lighting. The most popular choice for homeowners planning to stay long-term.

highEnd.title: Premium Transformation
highEnd.description: Complete gut renovation including structural changes, open-concept layouts, high-end kitchen and bathrooms, custom millwork, premium flooring, smart home integration, and designer finishes throughout. For homeowners who want a truly custom home.

factors.title: What Affects Your Whole House Renovation Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

scope.title: Scope & Square Footage
scope.description: The single biggest factor. A 600 sq ft condo refresh costs far less than a 2,500 sq ft house gut renovation. Define your must-haves vs nice-to-haves early to control budget.

kitchen.title: Kitchen (30–40% of total budget)
kitchen.description: Kitchens are typically the most expensive room. Custom cabinets, quartz countertops, and appliance upgrades can run $15,000–$72,000 on their own. See our kitchen cost guide for detailed breakdowns.

bathrooms.title: Bathrooms (15–25% of total budget)
bathrooms.description: Each bathroom renovation adds $10,000–$40,000 depending on size and finishes. Waterproofing, tile work, and fixture quality drive costs. Multi-bathroom homes see significant savings per unit.

flooring.title: Flooring (10–15% of total budget)
flooring.description: Engineered hardwood runs $8–$15/sq ft installed. Luxury vinyl plank (LVP) offers a budget-friendly alternative at $5–$10/sq ft. Tile for wet areas adds $12–$25/sq ft.

structural.title: Structural Changes
structural.description: Removing walls, adding windows, or reconfiguring layouts adds $5,000–$25,000+. Load-bearing wall removal requires engineering reports and permits. Open-concept conversions are popular but costly.

permits.title: Permits & Professional Fees
permits.description: Building permits in Metro Vancouver typically cost $500–$2,500 for whole house renovations. Budget an additional $2,000–$5,000 for architectural drawings, engineering reports, and inspections.

realProjects.title: Real Whole House Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed whole-house projects with actual budgets and timelines

included.title: What's Typically Included in a Whole House Renovation
included.subtitle: A comprehensive renovation touches every room and system

item1.title: Kitchen Renovation
item1.description: New cabinets, countertops, backsplash, sink, faucet, and updated appliance connections. Layout changes available for mid-range and premium tiers.

item2.title: Bathroom Remodels
item2.description: New vanity, toilet, shower or tub, tile work, waterproofing, and updated plumbing fixtures. Each bathroom fully refreshed or rebuilt.

item3.title: Flooring Throughout
item3.description: New flooring in all living spaces — hardwood, engineered wood, or LVP in main areas; tile in bathrooms and entryways.

item4.title: Paint & Trim
item4.description: Fresh paint on all walls and ceilings, new or refinished baseboards, door casings, and crown moulding where applicable.

item5.title: Electrical & Lighting
item5.description: Updated light fixtures, additional outlets where needed, dimmer switches, and panel upgrades for older homes.

item6.title: Doors & Hardware
item6.description: New interior doors, updated door handles and hinges, closet organizers, and any exterior door replacements as needed.

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips for Whole House Renovations

tip1.title: Prioritize and Phase Your Work
tip1.description: You don't have to do everything at once. Tackle the kitchen and bathrooms first (highest ROI), then address cosmetic updates in later phases. This spreads costs and lets you live in the home during renovations.

tip2.title: Keep Plumbing Where It Is
tip2.description: Relocating plumbing is one of the most expensive changes. Keeping kitchens and bathrooms in their current locations saves $5,000–$15,000 in plumbing and structural work.

tip3.title: Choose Materials Strategically
tip3.description: Splurge on high-impact, high-touch surfaces like kitchen countertops and bathroom tile. Save on areas like bedroom flooring (LVP looks great at half the cost of hardwood) and interior paint (mid-range paints perform nearly as well as premium).

tip4.title: Bundle for Better Pricing
tip4.description: Whole house renovations often cost less per room than doing rooms individually. Contractors offer better rates when they can schedule crews efficiently across a larger project.

faq.q1: How much does a whole house renovation cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Whole house renovation costs in Vancouver typically range from $50,000 to $200,000+ based on our completed projects. Cosmetic refreshes (paint, flooring, fixtures) start around $50,000–$80,000, comprehensive renovations with new kitchen and bathrooms run $80,000–$120,000, and premium transformations with structural changes can exceed $120,000.
faq.q2: How long does a whole house renovation take?
faq.a2: Most whole house renovations take 3 to 8 weeks depending on scope. A cosmetic refresh (paint, flooring, fixtures) can be done in 3–4 weeks. A comprehensive renovation including kitchen and bathrooms typically takes 5–8 weeks. Projects with structural changes or permits may take 10–14 weeks.
faq.q3: Can I live in my home during a whole house renovation?
faq.a3: It depends on the scope. For cosmetic refreshes, you can usually stay in the home as work is done room-by-room. For comprehensive renovations involving kitchen and bathroom demolition, we recommend arranging temporary accommodation for at least 2–4 weeks during the most disruptive phases.
faq.q4: What gives the best return on investment for a whole house renovation?
faq.a4: Kitchen and bathroom renovations consistently deliver the highest ROI in the Vancouver market. Updated flooring and fresh paint provide excellent value for relatively low cost. Open-concept layouts are popular with buyers but require structural work that increases costs significantly.
faq.q5: Do I need permits for a whole house renovation in Vancouver?
faq.a5: Yes, most whole house renovations require permits. Any work involving plumbing, electrical, gas, or structural changes needs a City of Vancouver building permit. Cosmetic-only work (paint, flooring, cabinets) typically does not require permits. Budget $500–$2,500 for permit fees.

cta.heading: Ready to Transform Your Entire Home?
cta.subtitle: Get a free consultation and detailed quote for your whole house renovation. We'll walk through every room and create a plan that fits your budget.

hero.title: Basement Renovation Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: What does it really cost to finish or renovate a basement in Greater Vancouver? Here are typical price ranges based on project scope — from basic finishing to full legal suite conversions.

tiers.title: Basement Renovation Cost by Tier
tiers.subtitle: Typical pricing for basement projects across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Basic Finishing
budget.description: Framing, insulation, drywall, basic flooring, paint, and standard lighting. Suitable for a recreation room, home office, or storage area without plumbing additions.

midRange.title: Mid-Range Renovation
midRange.description: Full finishing with a bathroom addition, upgraded flooring (vinyl plank or laminate), pot lights, built-in storage, and a wet bar or kitchenette. The most popular scope for family homes.

highEnd.title: Legal Suite Conversion
highEnd.description: Full secondary suite with separate entrance, kitchen, bathroom, bedroom with egress windows, fire separation, and all code-required features. Includes permits and inspections for rental legalization.

factors.title: What Affects Your Basement Renovation Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

waterproofing.title: Waterproofing & Moisture Control
waterproofing.description: Vancouver's wet climate makes waterproofing critical. Interior drainage systems run $3,000–8,000, while exterior waterproofing can cost $10,000–25,000. Sump pump installation adds $1,500–3,000.

framing.title: Framing & Insulation
framing.description: Steel or wood framing with insulation typically costs $8–15 per square foot. Older Vancouver homes may need additional vapor barriers and insulation upgrades to meet current building code.

plumbing.title: Plumbing (Bathroom/Kitchen)
plumbing.description: Adding a basement bathroom costs $8,000–15,000 depending on whether you need to break concrete for drain lines. A full kitchen installation adds $12,000–25,000 including rough-in and fixtures.

electrical.title: Electrical & HVAC
electrical.description: Basement electrical typically runs $3,000–8,000 for a standard layout. Many older homes need panel upgrades ($2,000–4,000). Extending HVAC ductwork adds $2,000–5,000.

egress.title: Egress Windows & Separate Entrance
egress.description: Building code requires egress windows in bedrooms ($2,000–5,000 each to install). Adding a separate exterior entrance for a suite costs $8,000–15,000 including excavation and concrete work.

permits.title: Permits & Code Compliance
permits.description: Building permits for basement finishing in Metro Vancouver cost $500–2,000. Legal suite conversions require additional inspections. Some municipalities offer grants for adding rental suites.

realProjects.title: Related Whole-House Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed renovation projects across Greater Vancouver — many include basement work

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips for Your Basement Renovation

tip1.title: Start with Waterproofing
tip1.description: Address any moisture issues before finishing walls. Fixing water problems after drywall is installed costs 3–5x more than doing it upfront.

tip2.title: Plan Plumbing Near Existing Lines
tip2.description: Locating your bathroom or kitchen near existing drain stacks saves $3,000–8,000 by avoiding concrete cutting and new rough-ins.

tip3.title: Consider a Legal Suite for ROI
tip3.description: A legal secondary suite in Vancouver can generate $1,500–2,500/month in rental income. The renovation often pays for itself in 3–5 years.

tip4.title: Choose LVP Flooring
tip4.description: Luxury vinyl plank is the best basement flooring choice — waterproof, warm underfoot, and 30–50% cheaper than engineered hardwood. Perfect for Vancouver's damp climate.

cta.heading: Get Your Free Basement Renovation Quote
cta.subtitle: Tell us about your project and receive a detailed estimate within 48 hours — no obligation.

faq.q1: How much does a basement renovation cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Basement renovation costs in Vancouver typically range from $30,000 for basic finishing to $120,000+ for a full legal suite conversion. The average cost is around $55,000 for a mid-range renovation with a bathroom addition and quality finishes. Cost per square foot ranges from $35 to $120 depending on the scope.
faq.q2: How long does a basement renovation take in Vancouver?
faq.a2: Most basement renovations in Vancouver take 6 to 12 weeks. Basic finishing (framing, drywall, flooring) can be done in 6–8 weeks. A full legal suite conversion with permits, plumbing, electrical, and inspections typically takes 10–14 weeks.
faq.q3: Do I need a permit for a basement renovation in Vancouver?
faq.a3: Yes, most basement renovations require building permits in Metro Vancouver. Any work involving framing, electrical, plumbing, or egress modifications requires a permit. Legal suite conversions need additional zoning approval. Permit costs range from $500 to $2,000 depending on the municipality.
faq.q4: Is a basement suite a good investment in Vancouver?
faq.a4: Yes, adding a legal secondary suite is one of the best renovation investments in Vancouver. A suite can generate $1,500–2,500 per month in rental income. With renovation costs of $80,000–120,000, the investment typically pays for itself in 3–5 years while also increasing your property value by 10–20%.
faq.q5: What is the best flooring for a basement renovation?
faq.a5: Luxury vinyl plank (LVP) is the most recommended basement flooring in Vancouver due to its waterproof properties and durability. It costs $4–8 per square foot installed and handles moisture better than hardwood or laminate. Engineered hardwood and ceramic tile are also good options depending on your budget and style preferences.

hero.title: Commercial Renovation Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: What does a commercial renovation really cost in Greater Vancouver? From small office refreshes to full restaurant build-outs, here are the real numbers based on our project experience.

tiers.title: Commercial Renovation Cost by Project Scope
tiers.subtitle: Typical pricing for commercial projects across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Small Retail / Office Refresh
budget.description: Fresh paint, new flooring, updated lighting, basic fixture upgrades, and minor layout changes. Ideal for small retail shops, professional offices, and co-working spaces that need a modern update without structural work.

midRange.title: Full Commercial Fit-Out
midRange.description: Complete interior build-out including new walls, custom millwork, upgraded HVAC zones, commercial-grade flooring, modern lighting design, and full electrical upgrades. Suitable for mid-sized offices, retail stores, and clinics.

highEnd.title: Restaurant / Medical / Premium Retail
highEnd.description: Full-scale renovation with commercial kitchen installation, specialized ventilation, custom fixtures, premium finishes, extensive plumbing, and complex permit requirements. Includes restaurants, dental clinics, medical offices, and flagship retail spaces.

factors.title: What Affects Your Commercial Renovation Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

permits.title: Permits & Zoning
permits.description: Commercial permits in Metro Vancouver cost $2,000–15,000 depending on scope and municipality. Change-of-use applications add $3,000–8,000. The City of Vancouver's development permit process can take 8–16 weeks, so factor in timeline costs.

hvac.title: HVAC & Mechanical Systems
hvac.description: Commercial HVAC is a major cost driver at $15,000–80,000+. Restaurant hood and make-up air systems alone cost $20,000–50,000. Office spaces need proper zoning for tenant comfort. Vancouver's energy code requires high-efficiency systems.

fireSafety.title: Fire Safety & Sprinklers
fireSafety.description: Fire sprinkler systems cost $3–8 per square foot to install or modify. Fire alarm upgrades run $5,000–20,000. Change-of-use triggers full fire code compliance review. Restaurants and assembly spaces have the most stringent requirements.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Compliance
accessibility.description: BC Building Code requires barrier-free access for commercial spaces. Accessible washrooms, ramps, door widths, and signage add $5,000–25,000. Heritage buildings in Vancouver often need creative solutions to meet accessibility standards.

electrical.title: Electrical & IT Infrastructure
electrical.description: Commercial electrical upgrades cost $10,000–50,000+ depending on service capacity. Restaurants need 200–400 amp services. Modern offices require structured cabling ($3,000–15,000), server room cooling, and dedicated circuits for equipment.

disruption.title: Project Timing & Business Disruption
disruption.description: Working around business hours increases costs 15–25%. Many Vancouver commercial tenants renovate during lease transitions. After-hours work adds premium labor costs. A phased renovation plan can keep portions of the business operational during construction.

realProjects.title: Our Commercial Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed commercial projects across Greater Vancouver

tip1.title: Plan During Lease Negotiations
tip1.description: Negotiate a tenant improvement allowance (TIA) with your landlord. Many Vancouver commercial leases include $20–80 per square foot in TIA that can offset renovation costs significantly.

tip2.title: Phase Your Renovation
tip2.description: Divide the project into phases to spread costs and minimize business disruption. Prioritize customer-facing areas first, then tackle back-of-house improvements in a second phase.

tip3.title: Reuse Existing Infrastructure
tip3.description: Keep plumbing and electrical in existing locations where possible. Moving a commercial kitchen or washroom can add $20,000–50,000 in rough-in costs alone.

tip4.title: Choose Durable Commercial-Grade Materials
tip4.description: Invest in commercial-grade flooring, fixtures, and finishes. Higher upfront cost saves money long-term — commercial LVT lasts 15–20 years vs. 5–8 years for residential-grade products in high-traffic spaces.

cta.heading: Get Your Free Commercial Renovation Quote
cta.subtitle: Tell us about your commercial project and receive a detailed estimate within 48 hours — no obligation.

faq.q1: How much does a commercial renovation cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Commercial renovation costs in Vancouver typically range from $50,000 for a small office refresh to over $1,000,000 for a full restaurant or medical facility build-out. The average commercial renovation costs around $250,000. Cost per square foot ranges from $75 to $300 depending on the type of business, scope of work, and finish level.
faq.q2: How long does a commercial renovation take in Vancouver?
faq.a2: Most commercial renovations in Vancouver take 8 to 20 weeks depending on scope. A basic office refresh can be completed in 8–10 weeks, while a full restaurant build-out or medical clinic conversion typically takes 16–20 weeks including permit approvals and inspections.
faq.q3: Do I need a permit for a commercial renovation in Vancouver?
faq.a3: Yes, nearly all commercial renovations in Metro Vancouver require building permits. Any work involving structural changes, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, or fire safety systems needs permits. Change-of-use applications are required when converting between business types. Permit costs range from $2,000 to $15,000 depending on the scope and municipality.
faq.q4: What is the cost per square foot for commercial renovation in Vancouver?
faq.a4: Commercial renovation costs in Vancouver range from $75 to $300 per square foot. Basic office refreshes run $75–$120/sq ft, mid-range retail or office fit-outs cost $120–$200/sq ft, and specialized spaces like restaurants or medical clinics range from $200–$300+ per square foot due to complex mechanical, plumbing, and ventilation requirements.
faq.q5: Can I renovate my commercial space while staying open for business?
faq.a5: Yes, phased renovations allow many Vancouver businesses to stay operational during construction. We create detailed phasing plans that section off work areas while keeping customer-facing spaces accessible. After-hours and weekend work is also an option, though it adds 15–25% to labor costs. Many of our clients choose to renovate during seasonal slow periods to minimize impact.

hero.title: Cabinet Refinishing & Refacing Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: A complete guide to cabinet refinishing, refacing, and replacement costs across Greater Vancouver. Based on our experience completing kitchen renovations with custom cabinetry throughout the region.

tiers.title: Cabinet Update Options by Cost
tiers.subtitle: Three approaches to updating your kitchen cabinets in Greater Vancouver

refinishing.title: Cabinet Refinishing
refinishing.description: Professional sanding, priming, and repainting or restaining of existing cabinet doors and frames. Best for solid wood cabinets in good structural condition. The most cost-effective way to transform your kitchen.

refacing.title: Cabinet Refacing
refacing.description: Replacing cabinet doors and drawer fronts while keeping existing cabinet boxes. Includes new veneer on visible surfaces and new hardware. A mid-range option that gives a completely new look without full demolition.

fullReplacement.title: Full Cabinet Replacement
fullReplacement.description: Complete removal and installation of new cabinets. Choose from stock, semi-custom, or fully custom options. Best when cabinets are structurally damaged or you want to change the kitchen layout entirely.

comparison.title: Refinishing vs. Replacement: Which Is Right for You?
comparison.subtitle: Understanding the trade-offs helps you make the best decision for your kitchen and budget

refinishing.title: Refinishing / Refacing
refinishing.pro2: Completed in 3–5 days with minimal disruption
refinishing.pro3: Eco-friendly — keeps existing cabinets out of landfill
refinishing.con1: Cannot change cabinet layout or configuration

replacement.title: Full Replacement
replacement.pro1: Completely customize layout, size, and configuration
replacement.pro2: Address structural issues and water damage
replacement.pro3: Modern features like soft-close and pull-out organizers
replacement.con1: 2–4 weeks of construction and higher cost

factors.title: What Affects Your Cabinet Refinishing Cost?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors that determine your final price

cabinetCount.title: Number of Cabinets
cabinetCount.description: A typical Vancouver kitchen has 20–30 cabinet doors and drawer fronts. More doors means more labour and materials. Expect to pay $150–$300 per door for professional refinishing.

material.title: Cabinet Material
material.description: Solid wood cabinets refinish beautifully. MDF and thermofoil cabinets require different techniques and may cost more. Laminate cabinets need special adhesion primers for lasting results.

finishType.title: Finish Type
finishType.description: Basic paint refinishing costs less than specialty finishes. Staining, glazing, or distressed looks add $500–$1,500 to the project. Two-tone cabinet designs are popular but require extra labour.

hardware.title: Hardware Upgrades
hardware.description: New handles, knobs, and hinges can add $300–$2,000 depending on quality. Upgrading to soft-close hinges is highly recommended and typically costs $5–$15 per hinge installed.

kitchenSize.title: Kitchen Size & Accessibility
kitchenSize.description: Larger kitchens with upper and lower cabinets, pantry units, and island cabinets cost more. Hard-to-reach cabinets or intricate moulding details increase labour time.

condition.title: Current Cabinet Condition
condition.description: Cabinets with water damage, delamination, or structural issues may need repairs before refinishing. Significant damage may make refinishing impractical — refacing or replacement becomes the better option.

realProjects.title: Related Kitchen Projects with Cabinet Work
realProjects.subtitle: Browse our completed kitchen renovations featuring custom cabinetry across Greater Vancouver

tip1.title: Consider Refinishing First
tip1.description: If your cabinet boxes are structurally sound, refinishing saves 50–70% over replacement. A professional paint job on solid wood cabinets can last 10–15 years with proper care.

tip2.title: Update Hardware for Instant Impact
tip2.description: New handles and knobs are the easiest and cheapest upgrade. Modern hardware on freshly painted cabinets can make your kitchen look completely renovated for a fraction of the cost.

tip3.title: Choose Durable Finishes
tip3.description: Invest in high-quality cabinet paint or lacquer. Benjamin Moore Advance or similar cabinet-grade finishes cost more upfront but resist chipping and yellowing far better than standard wall paint.

tip4.title: Bundle with a Kitchen Renovation
tip4.description: If you are already planning a kitchen renovation, bundling cabinet work with countertop and backsplash updates often results in better overall pricing and a more cohesive design.

faq.q1: How much does cabinet refinishing cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Cabinet refinishing in Vancouver typically costs $4,000 to $8,000 for a standard kitchen with 20–30 doors. This includes professional sanding, priming, and painting with cabinet-grade finish. Costs vary based on kitchen size, number of doors, and finish complexity.
faq.q2: How long does cabinet refinishing take?
faq.a2: Professional cabinet refinishing typically takes 3 to 5 days for a standard kitchen. The process includes removing doors, sanding, priming, applying multiple coats of paint or stain, and reinstalling with new hardware. You can use your kitchen throughout most of the process.
faq.q3: Is cabinet refinishing worth it or should I replace?
faq.a3: Cabinet refinishing is worth it when your existing cabinets are structurally sound and you are happy with the layout. It costs 50–70% less than replacement and takes days instead of weeks. Replace cabinets only if they have significant water damage, structural issues, or you need to change the kitchen layout.
faq.q4: What is the difference between cabinet refinishing and refacing?
faq.a4: Refinishing means sanding and repainting or restaining existing cabinet doors and frames. Refacing replaces the doors and drawer fronts entirely while keeping the existing cabinet boxes, adding new veneer to visible surfaces. Refacing costs more ($8,000–$15,000) but gives a more dramatic transformation.
faq.q5: Can you refinish laminate or thermofoil cabinets?
faq.a5: Yes, but it requires special preparation. Laminate cabinets need thorough deglossing and bonding primer for paint to adhere properly. Thermofoil cabinets can be painted but the thermofoil may peel over time. For these materials, refacing with new solid wood or MDF doors is often a better long-term investment.

cta.heading: Get Your Free Cabinet Refinishing Quote
cta.subtitle: Tell us about your cabinet project and receive a detailed estimate within 48 hours — no obligation.

hero.title: Basement Suite Conversion Cost in Vancouver BC (2026)
hero.subtitle: How much does it cost to build a legal basement suite in Greater Vancouver? Get detailed pricing for secondary suite conversions.

tiers.title: Basement Suite Cost by Tier
tiers.subtitle: Typical pricing for legal suite conversions across Greater Vancouver

budget.title: Basic In-Law Suite
budget.description: Converting an already-finished basement into a basic suite with kitchenette, bathroom, bedroom with egress window, and interior shared entrance. Minimal structural changes. Ideal for family use.

midRange.title: Standard Rental Suite
midRange.description: Full legal secondary suite with separate entrance, complete kitchen, full bathroom, one bedroom with egress, fire separation, and all code-required features. Most common scope for rental income.

highEnd.title: Premium Two-Bedroom Suite
highEnd.description: High-end legal suite with two bedrooms, full kitchen with quality appliances, in-suite laundry, upgraded finishes, separate HVAC zone, soundproofing, and premium fixtures.

factors.title: What Drives Basement Suite Conversion Costs?
factors.subtitle: Six key factors specific to legal suite builds

separateEntrance.title: Separate Entrance
separateEntrance.description: Most municipalities require a separate exterior entrance. Excavation, concrete stairs, drainage, landing, and fire-rated door typically cost $8,000–$18,000.

fireSeparation.title: Fire Separation & Safety
fireSeparation.description: BC Building Code requires 1-hour fire-rated separation between suite and main dwelling. Fire-rated drywall, smoke alarms, and fire-rated doors cost $4,000–$8,000.

plumbing.title: Kitchen & Bathroom Plumbing
plumbing.description: Full suite kitchen costs $12,000–$25,000 including cabinets, countertops, sink, and appliance hookups. Bathroom addition runs $8,000–$15,000.

electrical.title: Electrical Panel & Wiring
electrical.description: Suites need a dedicated sub-panel ($2,500–$5,000). Full wiring with code-required outlets and lighting costs $5,000–$10,000. Panel upgrades add $3,000–$5,000.

egress.title: Egress Windows & Natural Light
egress.description: Every bedroom needs an egress window (5.7 sq ft opening min). Installing in concrete foundation walls costs $2,500–$6,000 each. Window wells add $1,000–$2,000.

permits.title: Permits, Inspections & Legalization
permits.description: Permit fees range $1,000–$3,500. Expect 4–6 inspections. Vancouver and Burnaby offer suite incentive programs offsetting $5,000–$10,000 in costs.

roi.title: Return on Investment
roi.subtitle: Why a legal basement suite is one of the best renovation investments in Metro Vancouver

rentalIncome.title: Monthly Rental Income
rentalIncome.description: One-bedroom suites rent for $1,800–$2,200/month. Two-bedroom suites command $2,200–$2,800/month.

propertyValue.title: Property Value Increase
propertyValue.description: Homes with legal secondary suites sell for 15–25% more than comparable properties without.

paybackPeriod.title: Payback Period
paybackPeriod.description: At average rental rates, a $95,000 suite conversion pays for itself in 3–5 years through rental income alone.

realProjects.title: Related Renovation Projects
realProjects.subtitle: Browse {count} completed renovation projects across Greater Vancouver

municipalities.title: Suite Regulations by Municipality
municipalities.subtitle: Each Metro Vancouver city has different rules for secondary suites

vancouver.title: City of Vancouver
vancouver.description: Allows secondary suites in most single-family zones (RS). Affordable Housing program may provide incentives. Permits $1,500–$3,000.

burnaby.title: City of Burnaby
burnaby.description: Secondary Suite Program offers grants up to $10,000 for legalization. Requires minimum 1,850 sq ft lot. Suite max 40% of floor area.

surrey.title: City of Surrey
surrey.description: Allows suites in most single-family zones. Streamlined permit process. One additional off-street parking space required.

coquitlam.title: City of Coquitlam
coquitlam.description: Permits suites in RS-1 and RS-2 zones. Requires minimum lot size and separate utility meters. Pre-application consultations available.

tips.title: Money-Saving Tips for Your Basement Suite

tip1.title: Apply for Municipal Incentives First
tip1.description: Vancouver, Burnaby, and other cities offer grants for legal suite creation. Apply before construction — some require pre-approval. Savings: $5,000–$10,000.

tip2.title: Plan Kitchen Near Existing Plumbing
tip2.description: Locating the suite kitchen near existing drain stacks saves thousands. Avoiding concrete cutting saves $3,000–$8,000.

tip3.title: Choose a Side-Entry Entrance
tip3.description: Side-entry entrances cost significantly less than rear entries requiring major excavation. Savings: $5,000–$10,000.

tip4.title: Invest in Soundproofing Upfront
tip4.description: Resilient channel and insulation between floors costs $2,000–$4,000 during construction. After drywall: 3–5x more and less effective.

cta.heading: Get Your Free Basement Suite Quote
cta.subtitle: Tell us about your suite project and receive a detailed estimate within 48 hours — no obligation.

faq.q1: How much does a basement suite cost in Vancouver?
faq.a1: A legal basement suite conversion typically costs $60,000–$150,000. Basic in-law suite: $60,000–$85,000. Standard rental suite: $85,000–$120,000. Premium two-bedroom: $120,000–$150,000+. Average: ~$95,000.
faq.q2: Do I need a permit for a basement suite in Vancouver?
faq.a2: Yes, a building permit is required for any suite conversion in Metro Vancouver. Permits cost $1,000–$3,500. Expect 4–6 inspections. Operating without a permit risks fines, insurance issues, and liability.
faq.q3: How long does it take to build a basement suite?
faq.a3: Typically 10–16 weeks: permit processing (2–6 weeks), construction (8–12 weeks), and final inspections. Complex projects with structural work can take 16–20 weeks.
faq.q4: What is the minimum ceiling height for a basement suite in BC?
faq.a4: BC Building Code requires minimum 6 feet 5 inches (1.95m) for habitable rooms. If ceilings are too low, underpinning (lowering the floor) costs $15,000–$30,000.
faq.q5: Is a basement suite a good investment in Vancouver?
faq.a5: Yes — rental income of $1,800–$2,800/month means the ~$95,000 investment pays for itself in 3–5 years. Property value increases 15–25%. Suite income also helps with mortgage qualification.

hero.title: Renovation Financing & Budget Guide
hero.subtitle: Explore your financing options and plan your renovation budget wisely. Based on real project costs from our completed renovations across Metro Vancouver.

options.title: Financing Options for Your Renovation
options.subtitle: The most common ways Canadian homeowners fund renovation projects in British Columbia.

heloc.title: Home Equity Line of Credit (HELOC)
heloc.description: Borrow against your home equity with flexible draw amounts. Only pay interest on what you use. Available from most Canadian banks and credit unions.

personalLoan.title: Personal Loan or Line of Credit
personalLoan.description: Unsecured loans from banks or credit unions. No home equity required. Fixed monthly payments make budgeting predictable.

refinance.title: Mortgage Refinance
refinance.description: Refinance your mortgage to include renovation costs. Spreads the cost over your mortgage term. May have break penalties if mid-term.

savings.title: Savings & Phased Approach
savings.description: Pay from savings or phase your renovation into stages. Complete one room at a time to spread costs. No debt and no interest charges.

costs.title: Typical Renovation Costs in Metro Vancouver
costs.subtitle: Based on real completed projects from our portfolio.
costs.disclaimer: * Ranges based on our completed projects in Greater Vancouver. Actual costs vary by scope, materials, and location.

tips.title: Budget Planning Tips
tips.subtitle: Smart strategies to make the most of your renovation investment.

prioritize.title: Prioritize High-Impact Areas
prioritize.description: Kitchens and bathrooms offer the best return on investment. Focus your budget on spaces that matter most for daily living and resale value.

contingency.title: Set Aside 10–15% Contingency
contingency.description: Unexpected discoveries during renovation are common — old plumbing, hidden mold, or structural issues. A contingency fund prevents budget stress.

phased.title: Consider a Phased Approach
phased.description: If your total renovation exceeds your budget, prioritize and complete rooms in phases. This spreads costs while still achieving your goals.

quotes.title: Get Detailed Written Quotes
quotes.description: Always request itemized quotes from contractors. Compare scope of work, not just total price. A lower quote may exclude items you need.

permits.title: Budget for Permits & Inspections
permits.description: Building permits in Metro Vancouver typically cost $200–$2,000+ depending on the scope. Skipping permits can cause costly problems during resale.

roi.title: Think About Return on Investment
roi.description: Kitchen renovations can return 75–100% of costs at resale. Bathroom renovations return 60–70%. Basement suites can generate rental income to offset costs.

faq.q1: How much should I budget for a renovation in Vancouver?
faq.a1: Budget depends on scope: bathroom renovations range from $10,000–$60,000, kitchens from $15,000–$72,000, and whole-house projects from $50,000+. We provide free detailed estimates based on your specific needs.
faq.q1Answer: Budget depends on scope: bathroom renovations range from $10,000–$60,000, kitchens from $15,000–$72,000, and whole-house projects from $50,000+. We provide free detailed estimates based on your specific needs.
faq.q2: Can I renovate with a small budget?
faq.a2: Yes! We work with various budget levels. A phased approach lets you complete priority areas first. Cabinet refinishing ($3,000–$8,000) is a cost-effective alternative to full kitchen replacement.
faq.q2Answer: Yes! We work with various budget levels. A phased approach lets you complete priority areas first. Cabinet refinishing ($3,000–$8,000) is a cost-effective alternative to full kitchen replacement.
faq.a3: We follow a standard payment schedule: a deposit to secure your project date, progress payments at key milestones, and a final payment upon completion and your satisfaction.
faq.q3Answer: We follow a standard payment schedule: a deposit to secure your project date, progress payments at key milestones, and a final payment upon completion and your satisfaction.
faq.q4: What is included in a renovation quote?
faq.a4: Our quotes include labour, materials, project management, permits (if applicable), and cleanup. We provide itemized breakdowns so you understand exactly where your budget goes.
faq.q4Answer: Our quotes include labour, materials, project management, permits (if applicable), and cleanup. We provide itemized breakdowns so you understand exactly where your budget goes.
faq.q5: How can I maximize my renovation ROI?
faq.a5: Focus on kitchens and bathrooms for the best resale return. Use quality materials that last. Consider a basement suite for rental income. We help plan renovations that balance your lifestyle needs with long-term value.
faq.q5Answer: Focus on kitchens and bathrooms for the best resale return. Use quality materials that last. Consider a basement suite for rental income. We help plan renovations that balance your lifestyle needs with long-term value.

faq.q2: How long does a typical renovation take?
faq.a2: Renovation timelines vary by project scope. A bathroom renovation typically takes 2-4 weeks, a kitchen renovation 4-8 weeks, and a whole-house renovation 2-4 months. We provide detailed timelines during our free consultation.
